gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف أو مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/57)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف أو مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/57)
text: قُلْتُ لَهُ إِنَّ شِيعَتَكَ بِالْعِرَاقِ كَثِيرَةٌ وَ اللَّهِ مَا فِي أَهْلِ بَيْتِكَ مِثْلُكَ فَكَيْفَ لَا تَخْرُجُ قَالَ فَقَالَ يَا عَبْدَ اللَّهِ بْنَ عَطَاءٍ قَدْ أَخَذْتَ تَفْرُشُ أُذُنَيْكَ لِلنَّوْكَى إِي وَ اللَّهِ مَا أَنَا بِصَاحِبِكُمْ قَالَ قُلْتُ لَهُ فَمَنْ صَاحِبُنَا قَالَ انْظُرُوا مَنْ عَمِيَ عَلَى النَّاسِ وِلَادَتُهُ فَذَاكَ صَاحِبُكُمْ إِنَّهُ لَيْسَ مِنَّا أَحَدٌ يُشَارُ إِلَيْهِ بِالْإِصْبَعِ وَ يُمْضَغُ بِالْأَلْسُنِ إِلَّا مَاتَ غَيْظاً أَوْ رَغِمَ أَنْفُهُ .

en.hubeali: Al Husayn Bin Muhammad and someone else, from Ja’far Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Al Abbas Bin Aamir, from Musa Bin Hilal Al Kindy, from Abdullah Bin Ata’a,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup>, said, ‘I said to him<sup>asws</sup>, ‘Your<sup>asws</sup> Shias in Al-Iraq are a lot. By Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, there is none like you<sup>asws</sup> among your<sup>asws</sup> family members. So how come you<sup>asws</sup> are not coming out (rising against the ruling authorities)?’ So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘O Abdullah Bin Ata’a! You have taken the lining of your two ears for the silliness. Yes, by Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, I<sup>asws</sup> am not your Master (Al-Qaim<sup>asws</sup>)’. I said, ‘So who is our Master (Al-Qaim<sup>asws</sup>)?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Await the one whose birth the people would be blinded from, so that one would be your Master (Al-Qaim<sup>asws</sup>). There isn’t anyone from us<sup>asws</sup> who is pointed to by the fingers and chewed by the tongues except he<sup>asws</sup> has passed away in indignation or his<sup>asws</sup> nose was rubbed (pride was hurt)’.
en.sarwar: 26. Al-Husayn ibn Muhammad and others have narrated from Ja’far ibn Muhammad from Ali ibn al-‘Abbass ibn ‘Amir from Musa ibn Hilal al-Kindi from ‘Abdallah ibn ‘Ata’, fron abu Ja’far (a.s.) the following. “I said to him, ‘Your Shi‘a (followers) in Iraq are many. By Allah, there is no one like you in your family. Why do you not rise (for leadership)?” The narrator has said that he said, “O ‘Abdallah ibn ‘Ata’, you have began to open your ears to silliness. Yes, by Allah, I am not your companion (the one who will rise with Divine Authority).” The narrator has said that I said to him, “Who then is our companion (the one who will rise with Divine Authority)?” The Imam (a.s.) then said, “Find out whose time and place of birth is unknown to people, such person will be your companion (the one who will rise with Divine Authority). There is no one among us towards whom people point out their fingers so much and tongues will mention him so much, but that has died in frustration or due sorrow for being neglected.”
